skill.name: zentao-api
skill.description: 调用禅道（ZenTao）RESTful API v2.0 完成用户请求，覆盖项目集、产品、项目、执行、需求（Story/Epic/Requirement）、Bug、任务、测试用例、测试单、产品计划、版本、发布、反馈、工单、应用、用户、文件等 20 个模块的增删改查及状态流转操作。当用户提到禅道、zentao、查询项目进展、获取 Bug 列表、更新需求状态、创建任务等项目管理相关操作时使用本技能。
skill.summary: Integrate with ZenTao project management system via REST API v2.0 for project, bug, task, and requirement management.
skill.author: easysoft

security_audit.summary: All 628 static analysis findings are false positives. The skill is a legitimate ZenTao API integration tool for project management operations. Detected patterns (backtick syntax, weak crypto flags, high entropy) are misclassifications of markdown documentation and API parameter values. No malicious behavior confirmed after human review.

content.value_statement: Integrate Claude with ZenTao project management system to automate project tracking, bug management, and task workflow operations through REST API v2.0.
actual_capabilities: Query and manage projects, products, programs, and executions via ZenTao REST API v2.0
Create, update, resolve, and close bugs with automatic build reference handling
Manage requirements, epics, stories, and user requirements with state transitions
Create, start, finish, and close tasks with time tracking support
Query and manage test cases, test tasks, releases, and feedback items
Handle user authentication with secure token caching to local configuration file
limitations: Requires valid ZenTao server credentials or pre-configured access token
Cannot create execution without existing project context
Some API parameter names vary between modules (e.g., browseType vs status)

use_cases.title: Project Manager queries sprint progress
use_cases.description: Project managers can ask about active sprints, pending bugs, and task completion rates across their ZenTao projects without manually accessing the web interface.

use_cases.title: Developer creates and resolves bugs
use_cases.description: Developers can log new bugs, update bug status, resolve issues with resolution codes, and link bugs to specific builds directly through natural language requests.

use_cases.title: QA creates test cases and manages test suites
use_cases.description: QA engineers can create test cases, link them to products, manage test tasks, and track test execution results through conversational commands.

prompt_templates.title: Get project status
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me all active projects in ZenTao and their current progress including open bugs and pending tasks.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick status check for project monitoring

prompt_templates.title: Create and assign bug
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new bug in ZenTao for product {productID} with title "{bug title}" assigned to {developer}, severity {severity}, and mark it against the trunk build.

prompt_templates.title: Manage task workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Start task {taskID} that was assigned to me, and when finished, mark it as completed with 4 hours of work logged.

prompt_templates.title: Bulk status update
prompt_templates.prompt: Get all bugs with status 'active' in project {projectID}, then close the ones that were marked as resolved yesterday.

output_examples.input: Show me the top 5 bugs by severity in product 123
output_examples.output:
Here are the top 5 high-severity bugs in Product 123:

1. [P1] Database connection pool exhausted - Opened: 2026-04-25 - Assigned: developer@example.com
2. [P1] Login page crashes on mobile - Opened: 2026-04-24 - Assigned: mobile-team@example.com
3. [P2] File upload timeout - Opened: 2026-04-23 - Assigned: backend@example.com
4. [P2] Dashboard widgets not loading - Opened: 2026-04-22 - Assigned: frontend@example.com
5. [P2] Export feature broken - Opened: 2026-04-21 - Assigned: data-team@example.com

output_examples.input: Create a task in sprint 5 named 'Implement search feature' assigned to Alice with 8 hours estimate
output_examples.output:
Task created successfully in Sprint 5:

Task ID: 12345
Name: Implement search feature
Assigned to: Alice
Estimate: 8 hours
Execution: Sprint 5
Status: Open

best_practices: Configure ZenTao credentials as environment variables (ZENTAO_URL, ZENTAO_TOKEN) for secure re-use
When editing existing records, always GET current data first then PUT with merged changes
Use explicit grade parameter when creating Epic/Story/Requirement to avoid display issues
anti_patterns: Do not assume browseType works for all modules - Program and Task use 'status' instead
Do not skip user confirmation for write operations unless explicitly requested
Do not cache tokens indefinitely without checking for 401 errors on API calls

faq.question: What credentials do I need to configure?
faq.answer: You need ZENTAO_URL (server address) and either ZENTAO_TOKEN directly or ZENTAO_ACCOUNT plus ZENTAO_PASSWORD for login.

faq.question: How does token caching work?
faq.answer: After first login, credentials are saved to ~/.zentao-token.json. Subsequent calls use cached token without re-authentication.

faq.question: What if my token expires?
faq.answer: If you receive a 401 error, delete ~/.zentao-token.json and re-run the authentication to get a fresh token.

faq.question: Can I create bugs linked to multiple builds?
faq.answer: Yes, the openedBuild field accepts an array of build IDs such as ["trunk", "v2.1.0"].

faq.question: What is the difference between Epic, Story, and Requirement?
faq.answer: All three are requirement types with different hierarchies. Epic (BR) is highest, Story (SR) is standard, Requirement is for user-level needs.

faq.question: How do I close a requirement with a reason?
faq.answer: Use PUT /stories/{id}/close or /requirements/{id}/close with closedReason parameter. Valid values include done, subdivided, duplicate, postponed, willnotdo, cancel, bydesign.
